back_translation: He called His twelve disciples to Him and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast those demons out, and to heal every kind of disease and sickness.
The names of the twelve apostles are as follows: the first is Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his younger brother; James son of Zebedee and John his younger brother;
Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus and Thaddaeus;
Simon the Cananaean, and Judas Iscariot, the one who would later betray Him.
Jesus sent out these twelve disciples and commanded them, saying, "Do not go in the way of the Gentiles, and do not enter any town of the Samaritans;
but rather go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
As you go, proclaim, 'The kingdom of heaven has drawn near.'
Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse lepers so they are healed, cast out demons. You have received freely, so also give freely.
Do not procure gold, silver, or copper to put in your belts.
Do not take a bag for the journey, or two tunics, or sandals, or a staff; for the worker is worthy to receive his food.
Whatever town or village you enter, inquire who in that town or village is worthy, and stay there until you depart.
When you enter the house, greet it with a blessing.
If the house is worthy, let your peace come upon that house; but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
If anyone does not welcome you and does not listen to your words, when you go out of that house or town, shake off the dust from your feet.
Truly I say to you, on the day of judgment Sodom and Gomorrah will receive a punishment more bearable than that town.
Behold, I send you out like sheep in the midst of a pack of wolves; therefore be shrewd like serpents and pure-hearted like doves.
But beware of people, because they will hand you over to councils, and they will flog you in their synagogues;
and because of Me you will be brought before governors and kings, to be a testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
But when they hand you over, do not worry how or what you will speak, because in that hour one will give you what you should say.
For it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father speaks through you.
Brother will hand over his brother to death, and father will hand over his child likewise; children will rise up against their parents and cause them to be executed.
You will be hated by all people because of My name, but the one who endures to the end will be saved.
When they persecute you in one town, flee to another town; for truly I say to you, you will not yet have completed going through all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
A disciple is not above his teacher, nor is a slave above his master.

It is enough for a disciple to be like his teacher, and for a slave to be like his master. If they call the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more will they call the members of his house by worse names!
Therefore do not fear them. For there is nothing concealed that will not be revealed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.
What I speak to you in the dark, speak out in the light; and what you hear whispered in the ear, proclaim from the housetops.
Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. On the contrary, reverently fear Him who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
Are not two sparrows sold for one assarion? Yet not even one of them will fall to the ground without your Father's knowledge.
And even every hair of your head is already counted.
Therefore do not fear at all; you are of greater value than many sparrows.
Therefore whoever acknowledges Me before people, I also will acknowledge that person before My Father who dwells in heaven.
But whoever denies Me before people, I also will deny that person before My Father who dwells in heaven.
Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace but a sword.
For I have come to 'cause a son to be divided from his father, to cause a daughter to be divided from her mother, and to cause a daughter-in-law to be divided from her mother-in-law';
and 'his enemies will be the people of his own house.'
Whoever loves father or mother more than Me is not worthy of Me; and whoever loves son or daughter more than Me is not worthy of Me.
And whoever does not carry his cross and follow Me is not worthy of Me.
The one who finds his life will lose that life, and the one who loses his life for My sake will find that life.
The one who welcomes you also welcomes Me, and the one who welcomes Me also welcomes Him who sent Me.

The one who welcomes a prophet in the capacity of a prophet will receive a prophet's reward; and the one who welcomes a righteous person in the capacity of a righteous person will receive a righteous person's reward.
And whoever gives even just one cup of cold water to one of these little ones in the capacity that he is a disciple — truly I say to you, that person will not lose his reward at all."
